content.problemStatement:
Define the distance between 222 permutations‚Ä†^{\dag}‚Ä† AAA and BBB, each of length NNN, as the **minimum** value of ‚ą£Ai‚ąíBi‚ą£‚Ä°|A_i - B_i|^{\ddag}‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíBi‚Äč‚ą£‚Ä° for 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.

Find a pair of permutations of length NNN which have the **maximum** possible distance among all pairs of permutations of length NNN.

‚Ä†^{\dag}‚Ä† A permutation of length NNN is an array of NNN distinct elements which are all in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N]. For example, [2,3,1,4][2, 3, 1, 4][2,3,1,4] is a permutation of length 444 whereas [2,4,3][2, 4, 3][2,4,3] and [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2] are not.

‚Ä°^{\ddag}‚Ä° ‚ą£X‚ą£|X|‚ą£X‚ą£ denotes the absolute value of XXX. For example, ‚ą£‚ąí5‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ą£=5|-5| = |5| = 5‚ą£‚ąí5‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ą£=5.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN - the length of the permutations.


Output Format
For each test case, output 222 lines.



The first line containing NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, denoting the permutation AAA.
The second line containing NNN space-separated integers - B1,B2,B3,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, B_3, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, denoting the permutation BBB.

The permutations AAA and BBB must have the maximum distance possible. If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : There is only one possible permutation of length NNN, and thus only one possible pair, which has distance 000.
Test case 2 : The permutations [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and [2,1][2, 1][2,1] have a distance of 111. This can be shown to be the maximum possible distance.
